entities.name: CONSERVATION AGRICULTURE
entities.description: A farming approach advocated to enhance soil health and sustain long-term crop productivity

entities.name: SOIL HEALTH
entities.description: The condition of the soil, which conservation agriculture aims to enhance

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The yield and output of crops, which conservation agriculture aims to sustain

entities.name: SOIL COVER
entities.description: A key principle of conservation agriculture, often maintained by retaining crop residues

entities.name: CROP RESIDUES
entities.description: Plant material left on the field after harvest, used for mulch, feed, or fuel

entities.name: MULCHING
entities.description: The practice of retaining crop residues on the field to maintain soil cover

entities.name: SMALLHOLDER CROP-LIVESTOCK SYSTEMS
entities.description: Farming systems integrating crops and livestock, common in Africa and Asia

entities.name: SUB-SAHARAN AFRICA
entities.description: A region where the research on crop residue trade-offs was conducted

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: A region where the research on crop residue trade-offs was conducted

entities.name: VILLAGE SURVEYS
entities.description: The data collection method used in 12 sites across 9 countries

entities.name: POPULATION DENSITY
entities.description: A gradient used to cluster sites and assess residue management practices

entities.name: LIVESTOCK DENSITY
entities.description: A gradient used to cluster sites and assess residue management practices

entities.name: BIOMASS PRODUCTION
entities.description: The amount of organic material produced, affecting the availability of residues for mulch

entities.name: LIVESTOCK FEED
entities.description: One of the competing uses for crop residues, especially during the dry season

entities.name: FUEL RESOURCES
entities.description: One of the competing uses for crop residues

entities.name: DRY SEASON
entities.description: A period when farmers rely heavily on crop residues for livestock feed

entities.name: OPPORTUNITY COST
entities.description: The loss of potential gain from other alternatives when crop residues are used as mulch
